title: Get Your Free Life Insurance Quote | PolicyScanner
seo_title: Get Life Insurance Quotes Online | Compare 20+ Insurers | Free
seo_description: Compare life insurance quotes from 20+ top Canadian insurers. Free, instant quotes. Term, whole life, critical illness & more. Get your quote in 2 minutes.

_meta.target_words: 1,200–1,500 (plus the quote form — this is a CONVERSION page, not a reading page)
_meta.page_priority: MONEY PAGE — highest priority for SEO and conversion. Quote form must be above the fold.
_meta.design_note: Keep content lean. The form is the hero. Every section below supports conversion — it doesn't distract from it.

seo_keywords.density_rule: Use 'life insurance quotes' 2–3 times only. Vary heavily with 'rates', 'pricing', 'premium quotes' — this phrase stuffs easily.
seo_keywords.real_example_to_use: Michael, 38, from Mississauga — compared 5 insurers and saved $240/year. Use this example in the 'Why Compare' section.
seo_keywords.avoid: Do not write 'life insurance quotes canada' as a visible phrase — it reads unnaturally. Use 'life insurance quotes in Canada' or just 'life insurance quotes'.

page_data.title: Get Your Free Life Insurance Quote

_writer_notes.target_words: 40–60 only — keep it extremely tight above the form
_writer_notes.focus: Instant credibility + remove friction before the form
key_points: Lead with the value statement: Compare quotes from 20+ top Canadian insurers in minutes
Add 3-4 trust micro-bullets BEFORE the form (not after): ✓ Free, no obligation ✓ Results in minutes ✓ Licensed brokers to help you choose ✓ 100% confidential
DO NOT put long paragraphs above the form — every extra line hurts conversion
_writer_notes.example: 'Compare quotes from 20+ top Canadian insurers in minutes. Free, no obligation, and completely confidential.'

_writer_notes.focus: Create urgency and logic for comparing — show real dollar savings
key_points: HEADLINE STAT: 'Life insurance rates can vary by 30–50% between insurers for the same coverage.'
REAL EXAMPLE (include this verbatim or close to it):
By comparing, Michael saved $240/year ($20/month × 12) by choosing Insurer C.
• Each insurer has different underwriting guidelines
• Some specialize in certain age groups
• Some offer better rates for specific health conditions
KEY INSIGHT: What's best for one person isn't best for everyone.
CALLOUT: 'We represent 20+ insurers, so we can find the best rate for YOUR specific situation.'

page_data.title: Your Best Rate is Specific to You
page_data.text: The insurer that offers the best rate for your neighbour may not offer you the best rate. Age, health, and lifestyle all play a role. That's why comparing 20+ insurers — not just the biggest names — is the only way to guarantee you're not overpaying.

_writer_notes.focus: Remove confusion and fear — the quote process is simple and non-committal
key_points: Basic (for instant preliminary quote): Age, gender, smoking status, coverage amount, term length
For more accurate quotes: Height/weight, general health status, medications, family health history, occupation
PRELIMINARY vs FINAL QUOTE — explain the difference clearly:
Preliminary Quote (Instant): Based on basic info, assumes good health, gives price range, zero commitment
Final Quote (After Underwriting): Based on complete health info, may include medical exam, exact guaranteed rate
TIMELINE — write as a simple numbered flow:
3. Medical Exam (if needed) → scheduled within 1–2 weeks
5. Final Quote & Approval → coverage starts with first payment

page_data.heading: What Affects Your Life Insurance Quote?

_writer_notes.focus: Help readers self-assess — show them what they can and can't control
key_points: Women pay 20–30% less due to longer life expectancy.
Smokers pay 2–4x more. Includes cigarettes, cigars, vaping, marijuana.
Must be nicotine-free for 12 months to qualify as non-smoker rates.
Common conditions: Diabetes, high BP, high cholesterol, heart disease, cancer history, mental health, obesity (BMI 30+)
High-risk: construction, mining, fishing, skydiving, racing, scuba. May add 25–100% to premium.

page_data.heading: Top Canadian Life Insurance Companies We Work With
paragraphs: PolicyScanner compares rates from Canada's leading life insurers. Here are the major companies we work with and what each is known for.

bullets.text: Canada's largest insurer, excellent financial strength, top choice for participating whole life
Strong online process, Vitality wellness program, competitive for younger applicants
Wide product range, strong advisor network, result of merger of Great-West Life/London Life/Canada Life
Competitive for healthy applicants, simplified issue options, good for existing RBC clients
Canadian-owned mutual insurer, frequently among the most competitive term rates in Canada
Often lowest term rates nationally, especially for non-smokers, strong digital application process
Competitive for preferred health classes, strong western Canada presence
Easy online application, flexible term options, good for families
Desjardins, Foresters, Assumption Life, Humania, SSQ, La Capitale, UV Insurance, and others — each with unique strengths for specific health profiles and ages

_writer_notes.focus: Build confidence that we have genuine breadth — not just 3 or 4 names
key_points: Write a short intro paragraph before the bullets
After bullets, close with: 'Each insurer has different strengths. By comparing all of them, we find which one offers YOU the best rate for your age, health, and situation.'

page_data.heading: What Happens After You Request a Quote?

_writer_notes.focus: Set expectations clearly — reduce anxiety about what comes next after submitting the form
key_points: WITHIN 1 BUSINESS DAY: Licensed broker contacts you (email or phone — your preference), reviews your coverage needs, answers questions
WE PROVIDE: Detailed quotes from multiple insurers, side-by-side comparison, recommendation, no pressure to buy
3. Schedule medical exam if required (at your home or office)
6. Accept and pay first premium → coverage begins!
NO OBLIGATION NOTE: 'You're never obligated to buy. No fees for quotes or advice. Take your time.'
TESTIMONIAL: Include a real quote from a satisfied customer (e.g., 'I got quotes from 5 different insurers and saved over $30/month...')

page_data.title: No Obligation, Ever
page_data.text: Requesting a quote is 100% free with zero obligation to buy. Our brokers are paid by insurers when you choose to purchase — so there is never any fee to you for quotes, comparisons, or advice.

page_data.heading: Ready to Compare Quotes?
page_data.body: Get instant quotes from 20+ Canadian insurers. Free, fast, and no obligation. Or call us to speak with a licensed broker directly.

items.question: How long does it take to get a life insurance quote?
items.answer: Instant preliminary quotes online. Detailed broker quotes with full comparison within 1 business day.

items.question: Do I have to buy after getting a quote?
items.answer: No. Quotes are completely free with zero obligation to buy. There is no commitment involved in requesting a quote.

items.question: Will requesting a quote affect my credit score?
items.answer: No. Getting a life insurance quote does not affect your credit score. No credit check is run at the quote stage.

items.question: How accurate are online quotes?
items.answer: Online quotes assume you're in good health. Your final rate depends on complete health information and underwriting. Most people's final rates match preliminary quotes if they accurately report their health status.

items.question: Can I get a quote if I have health issues?
items.answer: Yes. We work with 20+ insurers, several of whom specialize in coverage for people with pre-existing conditions. You may pay higher rates, but coverage is typically available.

items.question: What information do I need for a quote?
items.answer: Basic: Age, gender, smoking status, desired coverage amount. For more accurate quotes: Height, weight, general health status.

items.question: Why use a broker instead of going directly to an insurer?
items.answer: Brokers compare 20+ insurers simultaneously. Going directly to one insurer means you only see that company's rates — which may not be the best for your age and health profile. Broker advice is free to you.

items.question: Is there a fee for using PolicyScanner's quote service?
items.answer: No. Our service is 100% free to you. Insurers pay us a commission when you purchase a policy. There is never a fee for quotes, comparisons, or broker advice.

_writer_notes.target_words: All FAQ answers are pre-written — review for tone and accuracy. Keep answers short (50–80 words each) and direct.
_writer_notes.focus: Address the most common objections and hesitations from someone about to submit a quote form
